string.name: cover_letter_template
string.value:
Dear [Recruiter Name],

I noticed your post about the [Role Title] opportunity at [Company Name] and wanted to reach out.

I am a Full Stack Developer currently at Adobe Inc. with 2 years of hands-on experience building production applications using React.js, Next.js, Node.js, and Express. I have also worked extensively with modern frontend tooling including Zustand, React Query, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI.

What stood out to me about this role is [specific aspect from the post]. My experience with [relevant tech] aligns well with what you are looking for, and I am confident I can contribute meaningfully to your team from day one.

I am based in Bhopal, open to relocation, and available to join immediately.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss this further.

Best regards,
Bibhuti Bhushan Saha

parameters.bodyParametersJson:
={"model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {{ JSON.stringify("Analyze the following LinkedIn post and determine if it is a recruiter or company advertising an actual job vacancy, OR if it's just a candidate looking for a job or sharing general advice.

LinkedIn Post by " + $json.recruiter_name + ":
" + $json.post_text + "

Reply ONLY with the word "YES" if it is a real job vacancy, or "NO" if it is not.") }} }], "temperature": 0.1}

string.name: prompt_text
string.value:
=You are a professional assistant crafting targeted job application cover letters.

Here is my profile:
- Name: Bibhuti Bhushan Saha
- Current Role: Full Stack Developer at Adobe Inc.
- Experience: 2 years
- Core Skills: React.js, Next.js, Node.js, Express, Zustand, React Query, HTML, CSS, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI
- Location: Bhopal (open to relocation)
- Availability: Immediate joiner

Below is my cover letter template:
---
{{ $('Define Configuration').item.json.cover_letter_template }}
---

LinkedIn post details:
- Recruiter: {{ $('Split In Batches').all(0, $runIndex)[0].json.recruiter_name }}
- Company: {{ $('Split In Batches').all(0, $runIndex)[0].json.company || 'their company' }}
- Post content:
{{ $('Split In Batches').all(0, $runIndex)[0].json.post_text }}

Tailor my cover letter template for this specific role. Follow these rules strictly:

1. Use the recruiter's actual name if it appears to be a real person's name. Otherwise use 'Hiring Team'.
2. Identify the specific role title from the post (e.g., 'Senior Frontend Developer', 'Node.js Engineer') and use it in place of [Role Title]. Never say 'the role' or 'this position' generically.
3. Replace [specific aspect from the post] with something genuinely notable about the opportunity — a product, technical challenge, team culture, or mission mentioned in the post.
4. Replace [relevant tech] with 1-2 specific technologies from the post that match my skills listed above. Be honest — only mention skills from my profile.
5. Keep the tone confident and professional, not desperate. No filler phrases like 'I came across your post', 'I am writing to express my interest', or 'I believe I would be a great fit'.
6. Keep the cover letter under 200 words. Brevity signals respect for the reader's time.
7. Format the cover letter with proper paragraph breaks using double newlines (escaped as \n\n in the JSON string). Ensure there is a blank line before the introduction paragraph, between body paragraphs, and before 'Best regards,\nBibhuti Bhushan Saha'.
8. Do NOT include the Candidate Summary section — it will be appended automatically after your output.
9. You MUST output your response as a valid JSON object with exactly two keys: "analysis" and "cover_letter".
Example output format:
{
  "analysis": "Brief 1-2 sentence analysis of what the role requires and which of my skills to highlight",
  "cover_letter": "Dear [Recruiter Name],\n\nI noticed your post about the [Role Title] opportunity at [Company Name] and wanted to reach out.\n\n[Paragraph 2]\n\n[Paragraph 3]\n\nBest regards,\nBibhuti Bhushan Saha"
}
